title: Rwanda Child Nutrition MEL Dashboard
cardImageAlt: Rwanda Child Nutrition MEL Dashboard preview
summary: Built and deployed an interactive Rwanda nutrition monitoring dashboard from UNICEF JME survey data to support evidence-based program tracking, trend interpretation, method comparison, and equity review for stakeholder reporting.
problem: Nutrition indicators were available in multi-sheet source files but not packaged into an accessible, decision-ready tool that answers four core MEL questions: (1) latest burden, (2) trend direction, (3) methodology differences, and (4) stratifier disparities.
approach: Ingested and cleaned JME workbook sheets (Primary Data, Age-Adjusted, Notes); modeled data in PostgreSQL with analytics views for snapshot, trends, method comparison, and stratifier analysis; built a Streamlit dashboard with five tabs (Overview, Trends & Change, Method Comparison, Equity/Stratifiers, Definitions & Limitations); added KPI cards with year-over-year deltas, insight callouts, consistent color mapping, and downloadable exports; and deployed to Streamlit Community Cloud with secure Neon PostgreSQL connectivity.
outcomes: Consolidated Rwanda nutrition monitoring into a single dashboard answering four core MEL questions across a 10-year analytical window (latest year: 2020).
Produced latest KPI snapshot: Stunting 33.1%, Wasting 1.1%, Underweight 7.7%, Severe Wasting 0.2%, Overweight 5.6%.
Added year-over-year signal layer: declines in stunting (-1.7 pp), wasting (-0.9 pp), underweight (-4.9 pp), severe wasting (-0.1 pp), and increase in overweight (+3.2 pp).
Enabled method comparison (Primary vs Age-Adjusted) with average gap insight and trend overlay for transparency.
Improved stakeholder readability with guided interpretation sections, definitions, limitations, and CSV export for offline use.

title: Clinical Operations & Patient Experience Dashboard (Synthea)
cardImageAlt: Clinical Operations and Patient Experience Dashboard preview
summary: Built and deployed a digital health operations dashboard combining open Synthea clinical records with synthetic patient feedback to monitor consultation volume, referral intensity, and experience signals (NPS, complaints, comments) for service quality review.
problem: Open clinical datasets rarely include complete patient-experience fields (NPS, complaint narratives), limiting full service-quality monitoring demonstrations.
approach: Loaded Synthea clinical CSVs (encounters, conditions, medications, procedures, patients, providers) into PostgreSQL; engineered analytics views for consultation records, monthly operations KPIs, and feedback aggregation; generated reproducible synthetic feedback records (NPS scores, complaint flags/categories, comment text) linked to encounters; built a narrative Streamlit dashboard with four tabs (Overview & Consultations, Operations Performance, Experience Signals, Comment Explorer); and deployed to Streamlit Community Cloud with secure Neon PostgreSQL connectivity.
outcomes: Modeled and analyzed 53,346 consultations across 6 encounter classes for operational monitoring.
Tracked service coverage with 1,171 unique patients and workforce activity with 1,104 unique clinicians.
Exposed operational pressure indicators including 25.8% referral rate for escalation analysis.
Added experience monitoring with synthetic survey layer: Avg NPS 4.97 and Complaint Rate 65.3% for quality signal tracking.
Enabled longitudinal review (1912-2020) with complaint-category and comment exploration for targeted action planning.

title: Life Prognosis Management Tool
summary: A patient management tool that estimates lifespan-related prognosis from HIV diagnosis context and provides actionable statistics to support administrative decision-making.
problem: Healthcare administrators and care teams needed a structured way to combine registration data and treatment timelines into a consistent prognosis and reporting workflow.
approach: Implemented complete system functionality including user login, patient registration confirmation, profile viewing and updates, prognosis calculation using diagnosis date, ART start date, and country-specific data, plus admin report exports and lifespan-related statistics.
outcomes: Enabled full patient lifecycle flow from registration to profile management within one platform.
Automated prognosis estimation logic from HIV-related patient timeline inputs.
Provided administrator-facing downloadable reports for user and lifespan statistics.
Improved consistency of decision support inputs through structured data capture.
Delivered a complete implementation from authentication to analytics output.

title: Mindora Conversational Therapy Chatbot
summary: A culturally aware conversational mental health support platform for Rwandan youth with multi-provider LLM support, emotion detection, safety controls, and context-aware therapeutic interactions.
problem: Mental health assistants often feel generic and culturally distant, while many implementations lack robust safety controls and reliable emotional context handling.
approach: Built and integrated a multi-provider AI architecture (Ollama, OpenAI, Groq, Hugging Face), advanced RAG with vector databases, real-time emotion detection, gender-aware response adaptation, safety and crisis checks, and production-ready FastAPI/React/PostgreSQL services with Docker-based deployment.
outcomes: Replaced keyword-based emotion handling with a context-aware LLM Emotion Classifier service.
Designed and implemented emotion logging models linked to users and conversations for longitudinal insights.
Enabled culturally grounded, gender-aware responses in natural Kinyarwanda addressing styles.
Integrated safety layers for crisis detection, content filtering, and ethical response guardrails.
Delivered scalable architecture with testing, CI/CD readiness, and containerized deployment.
privateDisclosure: This is a professional/work-based project. I can present architecture and outcomes upon request, but I cannot share the source code.

title: RAG System for Cancer Myth Detection and Fact-Checking
role: End-to-end implementation (architecture, retrievers, generators, evaluation, and interface)
summary: Built a configurable Retrieval-Augmented Generation system to answer cancer myth and fact-checking questions using verified medical evidence, with side-by-side comparison of retrieval and generation strategies.
problem: Cancer misinformation is widespread, while many QA systems provide responses without strong traceable evidence or clear benchmarking across retrieval and generation configurations.
approach: Implemented Streamlit and CLI workflows with modular retrievers (OpenAI embeddings with reranking and local SentenceTransformers+FAISS), modular generators (GPT-4o-mini and FLAN-T5), and a full evaluation pipeline including batch scoring, statistical analysis, status checks, and visualization for model comparison.
outcomes: Delivered an end-to-end RAG workflow for both interactive fact-checking and batch prediction runs.
Enabled reproducible comparison between cloud and local retrieval-generation stacks.
Added a complete evaluation suite for metric computation, aggregate analysis, and visualization.
Improved evidence-grounded answering for cancer myth detection by using verified medical source documents.
Packaged the system for experimentation, monitoring, and iterative model improvements.

title: Throttled Before the Vote: Network-Level Measurement of Social Media Interference Around Electoral Events in East Africa (2025–2026)
summary: An ongoing network measurement study analyzing social media interference patterns in Kenya, Uganda, and Tanzania around electoral periods using large-scale OONI measurements.
problem: Although election-period internet interference is increasingly documented, technical evidence on specific degradation mechanisms and timing dynamics remains under-characterized in empirical literature.
approach: Analyzes 52,676 OONI measurements collected between January 2025 and January 2026 across seven platforms (Twitter/X, WhatsApp, Facebook, Telegram, TikTok, YouTube, Instagram), classifying hard blocks versus soft throttling by mechanism and country context, then evaluating temporal escalation signals around key electoral milestones.
outcomes: Identified throttled DNS as the dominant interference mode across all three countries in the study window.
Separated hard-block events from soft-throttling conditions to improve interpretation of platform availability under interference.
Detected a statistically significant post-election hard-block escalation pattern around Uganda 2026 election day.
Produced a replicable, open-measurement methodology intended for future election monitoring studies.

title: Project Proposal: Enhancing Real-Time Kinyarwanda Sign Language Recognition and Translation
summary: An ongoing research proposal to improve real-time Kinyarwanda Sign Language recognition and translation into Kinyarwanda text with higher robustness and accuracy.
problem: Current KSL recognition approaches remain challenged by subtle sign differences, variable signing durations, and performance drops across diverse environmental conditions.
approach: Builds on prior MediaPipe + LSTM baselines and explores stronger sequence-modeling architectures and augmentation strategies to improve sign discrimination, temporal consistency, and real-world generalization.

title: Kaggle: Frame-Level Speech Recognition with MLP (MFCC to Phoneme)
summary: Built a multilayer perceptron pipeline for frame-level phonetic transcription from MFCC features in a speech recognition competition setting.
problem: The task required accurate frame-level phoneme prediction from raw acoustic features, balancing representation learning and robust class discrimination across phoneme labels.
approach: Developed and tuned an MLP-based classifier for MFCC frame inputs, optimized with cross-entropy loss and gradient-based training to improve phoneme-level prediction quality.
outcomes: Completed competition run for frame-level speech recognition (Aug 29, 2025 - Sep 20, 2025).
Achieved leaderboard performance of 84.908%.
Delivered a reproducible phoneme classification workflow for acoustic frame modeling.
Kaggle profile: https://www.kaggle.com/joeineza

title: Kaggle: Face Verification with Position-Invariant CNN Models
summary: Implemented a CNN-based face verification system to determine whether image pairs belong to the same person under positional variation.
problem: Face images are rarely perfectly centered, so verification models must remain reliable despite translational variation and other visual shifts.
approach: Trained and evaluated convolutional models for pairwise face verification, targeting robust feature learning and low Equal Error Rate (EER).
outcomes: Completed face verification competition cycle (Sep 20, 2025 - Oct 11, 2025).
Achieved EER score of 0.05715.
Strengthened model robustness for position-invariant visual recognition tasks.
Kaggle profile: https://www.kaggle.com/joeineza

title: Kaggle: Utterance-to-Phoneme Mapping
summary: Developed a sequence prediction approach for mapping speech utterances to phoneme outputs under edit-distance-based evaluation.
problem: The challenge required accurate phoneme sequence generation from utterance inputs while minimizing transcription distance from ground truth.
approach: Built and refined a speech-to-phoneme modeling pipeline with iterative validation against Levenshtein Distance competition metrics.
outcomes: Completed HW3P2 competition window (Oct 11, 2025 - Nov 8, 2025).
Achieved leaderboard score of 5.39694 (Levenshtein Distance metric).
Improved sequence-to-sequence transcription performance through iterative tuning.
Kaggle profile: https://www.kaggle.com/joeineza

title: Kaggle: Transformer-Based Automatic Speech Recognition
summary: Built a Transformer-based ASR system for transcript generation and optimization under character-level error metrics.
problem: Accurate end-to-end speech transcription requires strong contextual modeling and robust decoding under noisy acoustic variability.
approach: Implemented and tuned a Transformer ASR pipeline, iterating on training and decoding decisions to reduce Character Error Rate (CER).
outcomes: Completed HW4P2 ASR competition period (Nov 7, 2025 - Dec 6, 2025).
Achieved leaderboard performance of 26.55450 (CER metric).
Delivered an end-to-end Transformer transcription workflow for speech recognition tasks.
Kaggle profile: https://www.kaggle.com/joeineza

title: Rwanda Travel Planning Agent
role: Multi-Agent Orchestration, Critic Agent, and LangChain Integration Lead
summary: A RAG-enabled travel planning agent for Rwanda with multi-user group conversation support, evaluation workflows, and adaptive control for CMU Agentic AI coursework.
problem: Travel planning assistants often lack reliable grounding, coordinated multi-agent behavior, and robust group session handling for collaborative planning scenarios.
approach: Built an ORAS-based multi-agent architecture (Planner, Executor, Critic) using GPT-4o, OpenAI embeddings, Pinecone retrieval, and Supabase-backed session management; implemented orchestrator layers, agent message passing, critic-driven groundedness checks, decision policies (approve/revise/replan/escalate), and LangChain-compatible adapters with memory-enabled conversation chains.
outcomes: Implemented an integrated ORAS orchestrator with closed-loop planning and execution control.
Developed critic-driven groundedness evaluation with claim extraction and source verification.
Added adaptive decision routing across approve, revise, replan, and escalate actions.
Produced evaluation reports, trace generation workflows, and architecture scalability documentation.

title: CyberSec Buddy - AI-Powered Cybersecurity Learning Assistant
summary: An intelligent cybersecurity tutoring chatbot that helps beginners learn concepts through context-aware dialogue, semantic retrieval, and real-world examples.
problem: Cybersecurity learners often face heavy jargon, fragmented platforms, and limited real-world context, which slows practical skill development.
approach: Built a FastAPI + GPT-4 + ChromaDB assistant with conversation memory, platform-specific guidance (picoCTF, HackTheBox, TryHackMe), automated educational content processing, and retrieval-augmented explanations tied to real incidents.
outcomes: Delivered an interactive AI tutor tailored for beginner cybersecurity learning journeys.
Enabled semantic educational retrieval with context-preserving multi-turn conversations.
Improved accessibility of cybersecurity concepts with practical analogies and incident-grounded explanations.
privateDisclosure: This is a professional/work-based project. I can present architecture and outcomes upon request, but I cannot share the source code.

title: CareerTech Navigator System
summary: A comprehensive platform designed to bridge academia and industry at AUCA by connecting students with alumni expertise, personalized career guidance, and preparedness resources.
problem: Students faced an underutilized alumni network, limited structured career guidance, and gaps in professional readiness before entering industry.
approach: Designed and implemented a full-stack platform using a Vue.js frontend and Spring Boot backend to centralize alumni engagement, personalized support, and career-preparedness workflows.
outcomes: Created a centralized student-to-alumni guidance experience for AUCA career development.
Improved access to personalized career support and professional preparation pathways.
Delivered both frontend and backend systems as an integrated final-year project solution.
